codeReview.summary: The CLI tool implementation is complete and functional. It provides a standalone command-line interface (cmux-cli) that connects to a running cmux-linux instance via Unix domain socket and sends commands with formatted output. All 38 tests pass covering workspace, terminal, and focus commands. The implementation covers all expected behaviors from the feature description.

issues.description: json_get_string() is a minimal JSON parser that only handles basic escape sequences (\n, \r, \t). Complex JSON or special characters in workspace names could cause parsing issues. This is acceptable for the current use case but limits robustness.

Fixed buffer size obj[4096] in print_workspace_list() truncates large workspace objects. Data loss is silent (truncation only). Acceptable for typical use but could be improved with dynamic allocation.
Terminal output buffer is 65536 bytes. Very large terminal output could be truncated. This is a reasonable limit for most use cases.
Error message says 'is cmux-linux running?' but socket path is /tmp/cmux.sock. Minor inconsistency in naming, but non-functional.

summary: Review passed. The cli-tool feature implements a complete CLI tool (cmux-cli) with workspace, terminal, and focus command support. All 38 tests pass. The implementation follows the existing code patterns in the codebase (similar to test_*.c files) and properly integrates with the socket API. No significant issues found that would block the feature. The few non-blocking issues are related to JSON parsing robustness and buffer sizes, which are acceptable for the current use case.
